title: Sagenia subpedata
taxonomy: Sagenia subpedata is a species of fern in the family Matoniaceae. This plant is native to parts of Africa. It belongs to the genus Sagenia, which is a group of ferns commonly found in tropical regions. The Matoniaceae family is a small group of ferns that are typically found in tropical and subtropical regions of the world. Sagenia subpedata is classified as an evergreen fern, meaning it retains its leaves year-round.
